message:
fix(export): ensure SHA-256 hash is always computed for OPFS cache invalidation

Critical fix for GitHub Pages cache staleness (bv-pages-cache-fix issue).

Problem:
When exporting databases smaller than the chunk threshold, the config
file was written without computing the SHA-256 hash. This caused all
deployments to use the cache key "default", resulting in browsers
serving stale cached data even after updates.

Solution:
Refactor chunkIfNeeded() to always compute the SHA-256 hash FIRST,
before checking the chunk threshold. This ensures every export has a
unique cache key derived from the actual database content.

Changes to sqlite_export.go:
- Move hash computation before the chunk threshold check
- Reorder file operations: open → hash → decide on chunking → (optionally chunk)
- Improve file handle lifecycle with earlier Close() for non-chunked path
- All paths now produce a config with valid Hash field

Changes to sqlite_export_test.go:
- Add test assertions verifying Hash is always present
- Validate Hash is exactly 64 hex characters (SHA-256)

Changes to viewer.js:
- Add cleanStaleOPFS() function to garbage-collect old cache entries
- Called after successful cache hit to free storage from previous versions
- Cleans up both legacy "default" key and old hash-based entries

Version bump: v0.14.0 → v0.14.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
